body.title: Graph-tree memory — Igor's cognitive node store
body.summary: The SECOND kind of memory: Igor's RUNTIME COGNITION memory — a single node store (clan.memories, one Postgres table) where nodes relate two ways: a FOREST (parent_id hierarchy) and a GRAPH (interpretive_edges, Hebbian-weighted). Named traversals are cataloged as 'trees' (a query catalog, NOT the forest). Attractors are emergent hot regions, scored rather than labeled. Calving splits overgrown nodes (gated off by default). This is cognition memory (Postgres, NO SQLITE), distinct from the dev-process filesystem store.

key_concepts.name: 'tree' means three things
key_concepts.what: keep apart: the parent_id forest, the named-traversal query catalog, and tag/sensor trees — docs/memory_architecture.md §1

key_concepts.name: forest vs graph
key_concepts.what: parent_id = containment hierarchy; interpretive_edges = associative Hebbian graph; two relations over the same nodes

key_concepts.name: attractors
key_concepts.what: emergent hot regions surfaced by scoring (not pre-labeled); implemented ✅

key_concepts.name: calving
key_concepts.what: split an overgrown node into children when it accretes too much; designed + gated OFF by default 🔶

body.status_notes: node store / trees / attractors implemented ✅; calving gated off 🔶. Implemented-vs-designed gap map: docs/memory_architecture.md §4.
